 {"id":"bd-1ega.9","title":"Regenerate installer scripts from updated acfs.manifest.yaml","description":"## CRITICAL Task

After ALL manifest updates are complete, regenerate the installer scripts.

## Why This Is Critical

Per AGENTS.md, files in scripts/generated/ are AUTO-GENERATED from acfs.manifest.yaml.
Manual edits will be OVERWRITTEN. We must regenerate after manifest changes.

## Commands

```bash
cd packages/manifest
bun run generate
```

## Files Generated

- scripts/generated/install_*.sh
- scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh
- scripts/generated/manifest_index.sh

## Verification

```bash
shellcheck scripts/generated/*.sh
```

## Dependencies

This task should run AFTER all manifest.yaml updates but BEFORE E2E testing.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All generated scripts updated
2. shellcheck passes on generated scripts
3. +{"id":"bd-1eop","title":"Skip already-installed tools during install","description":"## Overview
Skip installation of tools that are already installed and at acceptable versions, making reinstalls and updates faster.

## Current Problem
- `acfs install` reinstalls everything
- Wastes time on already-installed tools
- No way to do incremental updates
- Full reinstall takes 5-10 minutes

## Proposed Behavior
- Check if tool exists in PATH
- Check if version meets minimum requirement
- Skip if both conditions met
- Show "✓ rust (already installed: 1.75.0)"

## Version Checking Strategy
```bash
check_tool_installed() {
  local tool="$1" min_version="$2"
  command -v "$tool" &>/dev/null || return 1
  local current=$("$tool" --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
  version_gte "$current" "$min_version"
}
```

## Manifest Extension
```yaml
tools:
  rust:
    check_command: "rustc --version"
    min_version: "1.75.0"
    install_script: "https://sh.rustup.rs"
```

## Force Reinstall Flag
```bash
acfs install --force  # Reinstall everything
acfs install rust --force  # Reinstall specific tool
```

## Output Format
```
Checking installed tools...
✓ rust 1.79.0 (skipped)
✓ bun 1.1.38 (skipped)
⚡ zoxide (installing...)
✓ zoxide 0.9.4 (installed)

Installed 1 tool, skipped 2 (already up to date)
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test version comparison function
- [ ] Test skip detection for all tools
- [ ] Test --force overrides skip
- [ ] Test partial install (some skip, some install)
- [ ] Benchmark time savings

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh (add version checks)
- scripts/install.sh (skip logic)
- manifest.yaml (add version requirements)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:01.258391752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:08:24.998724005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eop","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:13.540014972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ivl","title":"Smoke test issue - DELETE ME","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T16:17:21.411600172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:17:21.411600172Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1lug","title":"Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls","description":"## Overview
Implement exponential backoff for GitHub API rate limit errors to prevent installation failures during peak times.

## Current Problem
- GitHub API has rate limits (60/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr with token)
- Installation fetches many resources (releases, checksums)
- Rate limit errors cause hard failures
- No retry mechanism

## Proposed Behavior
- Detect 403 rate limit responses
- Implement exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s
- Show user-friendly message during wait
- Fall back to cached versions if available
- Suggest `gh auth login` if unauthenticated

## Implementation Details
```bash
github_fetch_with_backoff() {
  local url="$1" max_retries=5 delay=1
  for ((i=1; i<=max_retries; i++)); do
    response=$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o /tmp/response "$url")
    if [[ "$response" == "200" ]]; then
      cat /tmp/response; return 0
    elif [[ "$response" == "403" ]]; then
      log_warn "Rate limited, waiting ${delay}s (attempt $i/$max_retries)"
      sleep "$delay"
      delay=$((delay * 2))
    else
      return 1
    fi
  done
  return 1
}
```

## Rate Limit Detection
- HTTP 403 with "rate limit" in body
- X-RateLimit-Remaining header = 0
- X-RateLimit-Reset header for exact wait time

## User Communication
```
⏳ GitHub rate limit reached. Waiting 8s before retry (3/5)...
💡 Tip: Run 'gh auth login' for higher rate limits
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test backoff timing is correct
- [ ] Test max retries respected
- [ ] Test successful retry after limit clears
- [ ] Mock test for rate limit response handling
- [ ] Test fallback to cached versions

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/security.sh (fetch_with_checksum uses curl)
- New: scripts/lib/github_api.sh (GitHub-specific helpers)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:55.353096702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:08:12.255732767Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lug","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:08.229835256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
